finding_results.notes: Replaced repeated 'smell/smelled like' template constructions in the flagged spans with varied sensory phrasing led by temperature, touch, breath/airway irritation, and material texture while keeping scene pressure intact.

Recast the three impulse-control beats with distinct syntax patterns: involuntary vocal slip, deliberate send action with reflective follow-on, and externally triggered call initiation. Removed repeated 'before he could' hinge from the targeted beats.
Reworked side-character entrances to foreground action and speech rhythm within two sentences instead of repeating stacked physical-description templates.

summary: All three prose/copyedit findings were resolved with targeted line edits only: sensory-template repetition was reduced, impulse-control syntax was diversified, and character entrances were varied through action/dialogue-forward construction while preserving continuity and chapter voice.
